lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.original_text: It started as an academic exercise.
lines.sensory_text: The newspaper ad was three lines long. Five dollars a day. Jordan Hall basement. The young man circled it twice with a ballpoint pen, then folded the page in half.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.original_text: By hour thirty-six, prisoner 8612 was screaming.
lines.sensory_text: The sound came through the cinderblock at 2 AM. Not words. Just sound. The guard at the door adjusted his mirrored sunglasses and looked at his clipboard.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.original_text: Zimbardo watched through the observation window. He did not stop the experiment.
lines.sensory_text: Zimbardo pressed two fingers against the glass. On the other side, prisoner 8612 had stopped screaming. He was rocking. Zimbardo's notepad was open. He was writing.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.sensory_text: The cassette tape sat in a manila envelope for four decades. When a researcher finally pressed play, Zimbardo's voice filled the room, instructing the guards: be tough. Create fear. We need the data.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.original_text: That even scientists are not immune to the roles they create.
lines.sensory_text: The paper was retracted in 2023. In his office, Zimbardo's framed copy of the original study still hangs on the wall. The nail holds.
